scheme.scheme_name: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana

pages.title: National Portal for Rooftop Solar - Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
pages.text: National Portal for Rooftop Solar - Ministry of New and Renewable Energy PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana "In order to further sustainable development and people's well-being, we are launching the PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana. This project, with an investment of over Rs. 75,000 crores, aims to light up 1 crore households by providing up to 300 units of free electricity every month." Shri Narendra Modi Hon’ble Prime Minister of India TIME LEFT TO GET BENEFIT OF SCHEME Scheme closes on 31 March 2027 290 DAYS 06 HOURS 09 MINS 46 SECS SCHEME ACHIEVEMENT AT A GLANCE – AS ON 13 JUN 2026 67,48,365 Total Application Updated Daily 32,21,323 Total RTS Installations Updated Daily 39,33,236 Household Benefitted Updated Daily Announcement 1. The provision allowing installation submission of applications (by vendor) with repeated module/inverter serial numbers based on vendor undertakings will be discontinued from 26 May 2026. What's New View More Shri Pralhad Venkatesh Joshi Hon’ble Cabinet Minister Shri Shripad Yesso Naik Hon’ble Minister of State What's New 8 Jun Public Notice - Clarification on Exemption from ALMM List-II for RTS projects under ‘Give it Up’ category of PMSG:MBY General Notices 25 May Public Notice - Advisory dated 21.05.2026 regarding timely commissioning of RTS projects General Notices 25 May Public Notice - Expression of Interest for a Market Assessment Exercise on AI based retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) Systems. General Notices 20 May Public Notice - OM dated-19.05.2026 regarding Amendment in Technical Specifications for Rooftop Solar Plants Installed under the Component of “CFA to Residential Consumers” of PMSG MBY General Notices 11 May Public Notice - OM-11.05.2026-Discontinuation of the provision allowing applications with repeated solar module/inverter serial numbers based on undertaking from vendors under PM-Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana General Notices View More Benefits Subsidy for Residential Households Rs. 30,000 per kW up to 2 kW Rs. 18,000 per kW for additional capacity up to 3 kW Rs. 78,000 Total subsidy for systems larger than 3 kW capped at Subsidy for GHS/RWA (Group Housing Society/Resident Welfare Association) Rs. 18,000 per kW for common facilities, including EV charging, up to 500 kW Capacity(@3 kW per house) with the upper limit being inclusive of individual rooftop plants installed by individual residents in the GHS/RWA For special states, an additional 10% Subsidy will be applicable per kW Suitable Rooftop Solar Plant Capacity for households Average Monthly Electricity Consumption (units) 0-150 150-300 >300 Suitable Rooftop Solar Plant Capacity 1-2 kw 2-3 kw Above 3 kw For special states, an additional 10% Subsidy will be applicable per kW Suitable Rooftop Solar Plant Capacity for households Average Monthly Electricity Consumption (units) 0-150 150-300 >300 Suitable Rooftop Solar Plant Capacity 1-2 kw 2-3 kw Above 3 kw Average Monthly Electricity Consumption (units) Suitable Rooftop Solar Plant Capacity 0-150 1-2 kw 150-300 2-3 kw >300 Above 3 kw Consumer Registration Process Step 01 Visit the website at https://pmsuryaghar.gov.in/ Step 02 Go to consumer page and select “Apply Now” (or) Open the Login dropdown menu and select “Consumer Login”. Step 03 Enter valid Registered Consumer Mobile Number for Registration, Enter valid Captcha and Select “Yes, I have read all the guidelines of PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana.”, then Click on “Verify” Step 04 Enter valid Mobile OTP received through SMS and then click on “Login”. Step 05 After successful Login, then Enter Profile details of Registration Consumer by entering valid Consumer Name, E-Mail, Address, State, District and PIN Code and then click on “Save”. Step 06 Registered consumer can submit applications either through click on “Apply for Solar Rooftop” (or) through Vendor Selection. Consumer can apply for Solar Rooftop by selecting State, District and Electricity Distribution Company/ Utility and enter Consumer account number, then click on “Fetch Details”. After loading the Consumer details, then click on “Next” to start filling for submission of application.· Step 01 Visit the website at https://pmsuryaghar.gov.in/ Step 02 Go to consumer page and select “Apply Now” (or) Open the Login dropdown menu and select “Consumer Login”. Step 03 Enter valid Registered Consumer Mobile Number for Registration, Enter valid Captcha and Select “Yes, I have read all the guidelines of PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana.”, then Click on “Verify” Step 04 Enter valid Mobile OTP received through SMS and then click on “Login”. Step 05 After successful Login, then Enter Profile details of Registration Consumer by entering valid Consumer Name, E-Mail, Address, State, District and PIN Code and then click on “Save”. Step 06 Registered consumer can submit applications either through click on “Apply for Solar Rooftop” (or) through Vendor Selection. Consumer can apply for Solar Rooftop by selecting State, District and Electricity Distribution Company/ Utility and enter Consumer account number, then click on “Fetch Details”. After loading the Consumer details, then click on “Next” to start filling for submission of application.·

analysis.overview: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana is a Government of India scheme launched to promote rooftop solar installations in residential households. With an investment of over Rs. 75,000 crores, it aims to provide up to 300 units of free electricity per month to 1 crore households. The scheme offers financial subsidies for solar rooftop systems and enables consumers to benefit from reduced electricity bills through solar energy generation.
objectives: Promote sustainable development and people's well-being through solar energy adoption
Provide up to 300 units of free electricity per month to beneficiary households
Install rooftop solar systems in 1 crore households across India
Reduce dependence on grid electricity and lower household electricity expenses
Encourage renewable energy usage at the residential level
analysis.eligibility: Residential households across India are eligible to apply for the PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana. Applicants must register on the national portal and provide valid consumer details including mobile number, name, email, address, state, district, PIN code, and electricity consumer account number. Applications can be submitted either directly by the consumer or through empanelled vendors.
analysis.benefits: Financial subsidy for residential households: Rs. 30,000 per kW up to 2 kW, Rs. 18,000 per kW for additional capacity up to 3 kW, and a total subsidy of Rs. 78,000 for systems larger than 3 kW. For Group Housing Societies (GHS) and Resident Welfare Associations (RWA), subsidy is Rs. 18,000 per kW for common facilities (including EV charging) up to 500 kW capacity. Special states receive an additional 10% subsidy per kW. Beneficiaries also receive up to 300 units of free electricity monthly after installation.
analysis.financial_support: The scheme provides a central financial subsidy for rooftop solar installations. For individual households: Rs. 30,000 per kW for the first 2 kW, Rs. 18,000 per kW for the next kW (up to 3 kW), and a capped total subsidy of Rs. 78,000 for systems above 3 kW. For GHS/RWA, Rs. 18,000 per kW is provided for common facilities up to 500 kW. Special states receive an additional 10% subsidy per kW. The subsidy is disbursed after successful installation and verification.
analysis.grant_amount_per_entity: Rs. 78,000 (capped subsidy for systems above 3 kW)
analysis.application_process: Step 01: Visit the website at https://pmsuryaghar.gov.in/. Step 02: Go to consumer page and select 'Apply Now' or open the Login dropdown and select 'Consumer Login'. Step 03: Enter valid registered consumer mobile number, enter captcha, select 'Yes, I have read all the guidelines of PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana', then click 'Verify'. Step 04: Enter valid mobile OTP received via SMS and click 'Login'. Step 05: After successful login, enter profile details (consumer name, email, address, state, district, PIN code) and click 'Save'. Step 06: Submit application either by clicking 'Apply for Solar Rooftop' or through vendor selection. Select state, district, electricity distribution company/utility, enter consumer account number, click 'Fetch Details', then click 'Next' to start filling the application.
analysis.implementing_agency: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
caveats: The provision allowing installation submission of applications with repeated module/inverter serial numbers based on vendor undertakings will be discontinued from 26 May 2026
Subsidy for systems larger than 3 kW is capped at Rs. 78,000
Additional 10% subsidy per kW is applicable only for special states
